title: genista insularis subsp. fodinae
intro: Genista insularis subsp. fodinae, also known as the Azores broom or broom, is a subspecies of shrub native to the island of São Miguel in the Azores archipelago. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a diuretic. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and acting as an antiseptic, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Genista insularis subsp. fodinae is often used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
